userStories.title: Fix parallel batch metrics aggregation (BUG-064, BUG-065, BUG-066)
userStories.description:
The runner only tracks metrics from the sequential execution path. After a parallel batch completes, the runner does not add the batch cost, completed story count, or per-story metrics into its running totals. Fix three bugs in runner.ts and parallel-executor.ts:

(1) BUG-064 - totalCost: After parallel-executor returns, add the returned batchCost to the runner cost accumulator before starting sequential execution. The run.complete event must include combined cost from both paths.

(2) BUG-065 - storiesCompleted: After a parallel batch, increment the runner storiesCompleted counter by the number of stories that were successfully merged (not just pipeline-passed). Parallel stories must appear in the final storiesCompleted count in run.complete.

(3) BUG-066 - storyMetrics: The parallel-executor already has per-story data (story id, cost, duration, attempts, firstPassSuccess) from each story pipeline run. Return this as a structured array from parallel-executor. In runner.ts, merge the parallel story metrics into the storyMetrics array alongside sequential story metrics. Each entry should have a source field ('parallel' | 'sequential') to distinguish origin.

All three bugs are in the data handoff between parallel-executor.ts and runner.ts. Add/update unit tests for the runner metric aggregation to cover the parallel + sequential combined scenario.

routing.reasoning: Three interconnected metric aggregation bugs with clear data-flow fixes across two files; straightforward data plumbing logic with moderate test complexity for parallel+sequential combined scenario.

userStories.title: Preserve story metrics across model escalation (BUG-067)
userStories.description:
When a story escalates from one model tier to another (e.g., haiku to sonnet after test failure), the escalation handler resets the story accumulated metrics - cost, attempt count, and firstPassSuccess flag are overwritten by the new attempt data. Fix the escalation metrics accumulator:

(1) Cost - track totalAttemptCost that accumulates across all attempts (including failed ones). The final storyMetrics entry should show the full cost of all execution, not just the successful attempt.

(2) Attempts - the attempt counter must NOT reset on escalation. If haiku runs once (attempt 1, fails) and sonnet runs once (attempt 2, succeeds), storyMetrics must report attempts: 2.

(3) firstPassSuccess - set this flag based on the result of the FIRST verify call. It must not be overwritten when a subsequent attempt succeeds. If the first attempt failed (even due to escalation), firstPassSuccess must be false.

The fix is in the escalation handler and/or the story metrics accumulator (likely in src/execution/escalation/ and src/pipeline/stages/execution.ts). Identify where metrics are reset on escalation and preserve them across retry. Add unit tests covering the escalation -> retry -> success scenario with correct metric assertions.

userStories.title: Parallel executor cleanup: duplicate log, batch summary fields, duration naming (BUG-068, BUG-069, BUG-071)
userStories.description:
Three low-severity cleanup fixes across parallel-executor.ts and runner.ts:

(1) BUG-068 - Remove duplicate 'Parallel execution complete' log line. Two identical log lines emit at the same timestamp - likely one at the end of the merge loop and one at function return. Keep exactly one.

(2) BUG-069 - Fix batch summary field semantics. The 'successful' count currently conflates pipeline-pass with merge-success. A story that passes TDD but conflicts on merge should not be counted as 'successful'. Rename 'successful' to 'pipelinePassed'. Add a 'merged' field for stories that were actually merged to the base branch. 'mergeConflicts' stays as-is. Summary: { pipelinePassed, merged, mergeConflicts, failed }.

(3) BUG-071 - Fix story.complete duration field naming. Currently there are two confusingly named fields: durationMs (elapsed since run start, not story start) and storyDurationMs (actual story wall-clock time). Rename durationMs to runElapsedMs to clarify it is elapsed since run start, not story duration. Update any downstream consumers (reporters, formatters, UI) that read this field.

Update relevant tests to match renamed fields. No behavioral changes - purely naming and deduplication.

userStories.title: Classify runtime crashes separately from test failures to prevent spurious escalation (BUG-070)
userStories.description:
When the verify stage detects test failure, it triggers escalation (e.g., haiku to sonnet). But some failures are actually Bun runtime crashes (segfaults, panics), not code errors. Escalating the model tier is useless for runtime crashes.

Fix in verify.ts (or the verify stage output handler):

(1) Parse the test runner output for known crash patterns: 'panic(main thread)', 'Segmentation fault', 'Bun has crashed', 'oh no: Bun has crashed'. These are emitted by Bun itself before any test result.

(2) If a crash pattern is detected, classify the result as RUNTIME_CRASH instead of TEST_FAILURE.

(3) In the escalation handler, add a branch: if verifyResult.failureReason === RUNTIME_CRASH -> retry the SAME tier (transient failure, not a code quality issue). Only escalate on genuine TEST_FAILURE.

(4) Add a runtimeCrashes counter to story metrics. If a story retried due to runtime crashes, log it and include in storyMetrics.

Add unit tests for the crash detection pattern matching, and integration tests for the escalation path branching (crash -> retry-same vs failure -> escalate).

userStories.title: Merge conflict rectification: sequential re-run of conflicted stories on updated base
userStories.description:
When parallel stories conflict during merge, they are currently marked as failed and require manual resolution. Implement automatic rectification: conflicted stories re-run sequentially on the updated base branch (which includes all successfully merged stories), giving the agent full context of what already exists.

Implementation in src/execution/parallel-executor.ts:

After the first merge pass (where some stories may conflict), collect a retryQueue of conflicted stories. For each story in retryQueue, sequentially:
  1. Remove the old worktree (git worktree remove --force .nax-wt/<storyId>)
  2. Create a fresh worktree from current HEAD (which has all clean merges)
  3. Re-run the full story pipeline (routing -> prompt -> execution -> verify) - same as sequential executor
  4. Attempt merge again on the now-updated base
  5. If it conflicts again, preserve worktree and mark as finalConflict (structural issue, cannot auto-resolve)
  6. If it succeeds, mark as rectified and include in merged count

Rectification runs strictly sequentially so each rectified story sees the previous one merge.

Cost tracking: each rectified story must track both originalCost (the failed parallel run) and rectificationCost (the re-run). Include both in storyMetrics with source: 'rectification' and a rectifiedFromConflict: true flag.

Logging (prefix [parallel]):
  - 'Merge conflict detected - scheduling for rectification {storyId, conflictFiles}'
  - 'Starting merge conflict rectification {stories, totalConflicts}'
  - 'Rectifying story on updated base {storyId, attempt: rectification}'
  - 'Rectification succeeded - story merged {storyId, originalCost, rectificationCost}'
  - 'Rectification failed - preserving worktree {storyId}'
  - 'Rectification complete {rectified, stillConflicting}'

Edge cases: rectified story fails verification (not merge conflict) -> treat as normal pipeline failure, escalate if configured.

Update run summary to report rectification stats. Add integration tests for the rectification flow: parallel batch with conflict -> rectification pass -> successful merge.
